info.title: Fiber AI API
info.description:
# Welcome to the Fiber AI API!



Get your API key at <https://fiber.ai/app/api>.

## Authentication

Include your API key in the request body for POST requests or in the query params for GET requests.

For example, for a POST request, you can include your API key in the request body like this:
```json
{
"apiKey": "YOUR_API_KEY"
}
```

For a GET request, you can include your API key in the query params like this:
```
?apiKey=YOUR_API_KEY
```

## Rate limits

Each endpoint has its own rate limit, and if you exceed it you will get a '429' error. Please contact us if you'd like to request a higher rate limit.

## Troubleshooting

Need help? Write to adi@fiber.ai or your contact on the Fiber team.

# SDKs & MCP

Integrate the Fiber API into your applications with official SDKs or via **MCP** (Model Context Protocol) for LLM and AI agents.

- **[Fiber SDKs](https://docs.fiber.ai/article/fiber-sdks)** — Client libraries and usage guides for your language or framework.
- **[Using MCP in LLMs](https://docs.fiber.ai/article/using-mcp-in-llms)** — Connect Claude, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible tools to Fiber.
- **[Developing with AI agents](https://docs.fiber.ai/article/developing-with-ai-agents)** — Plugins, MCP servers, skills, and quickstart for building with Fiber in your IDE.

---

post.description: Polls a batch contact enrichment task. Returns partial results as they complete. Call this endpoint repeatedly until the 'done' field is true. Use the task ID returned from the 'Start batch contact enrichment' endpoint. ⚡ Rate limit: 10 requests per 1 minute

taskId.description: The task ID from the start endpoint response

cursor.description: The cursor from the previous poll response. Omit this if you are polling for the first time.

take.description: Number of people to take per batch. Default is 100

totalPeopleToFetch.description: Total number of people in this bulk task

numCompleted.description: Number of people for which contact reveal process has been completed.

numRemaining.description: Number of people remaining to be processed

numRejected.description: Number of people for which contact reveal process has been rejected either due to invalid input or person not found

numDuplicates.description: Number of duplicates found in the input

overallStats.description: Overall statistics for the batch enrichment task

done.description: True when ALL people have been processed (completed or failed)

inputs.description: The input details provided for this person

outputs.description: The reveal result for this person

pageResults.description: Array of results for each person in the current page. You need to keep paginating using the cursor to get more results.

nextCursor.description: Next cursor to use for pagination. Use this to get the next page of results.

cursor.description: The cursor from where to start fetching the next page of results. Provide the `nextCursor` from the previous response to continue from there.

pageSize.description: The number of results to fetch per page.

fullName.description: The full name of the person this result corresponds to. This generally matches the input full name.

githubUrl.description: The GitHub profile URL that best matches this person. Null if we could not confidently find a profile.

username.description: The GitHub username (login) for the matched profile. Null when no confident match was found.

displayName.description: The user's display name on GitHub.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's GitHub profile picture.

bio.description: One-line summary below the person's name on GitHub.

location.description: Location as displayed on the GitHub profile.

confidenceOutOf10.description: Confidence score between 1 and 10 denoting the match quality. Higher scores mean a stronger match.

rationale.description: The reasoning for why the agent chose (or did not choose) the given GitHub profile.

errorMessage.description: Error message if the lookup failed for this person.

customerProvidedId.description: The customerProvidedId echoed back from the input so you can easily join results to your original dataset.

data.description: The list of GitHub profiles found along with their rationale and confidence scores.

nextCursor.description: The cursor to the next page of results. Provide this to the polling endpoint to get the next page of results - null if there are no more results.

hasMore.description: Whether there are more results to fetch for this run.

countryCodeAlpha2.description: ISO country code (e.g., 'US'). This is a 2 letter code.

subZones.description: Related timezone variants that share geographic proximity or similar time offset behaviors (e.g., Mountain Time zones in British Columbia: 'America/Creston', 'America/Dawson_Creek', 'America/Fort_Nelson')

currentLocalTime.description: Current time in this timezone, formatted as an ISO 8601 string.

isDaylightSaving.description: Whether the time zone is currently in Daylight Savings time.

post.description: Search for investors with flexible filtering capabilities ⚡ Rate limit: 30 requests per 1 minute 💰 Cost: 3 credits per investor found ⓘ

countryCode.description: Country or region filter with include/exclude pattern. Region codes like X-ANGLOSPHERE expand to multiple countries (USA, GBR, AUS, etc.)

stateName.description: State name filter with include/exclude pattern. Use full state names (e.g., 'California', 'England', 'New York'). State codes like 'CA' or 'NY' are not accepted.

investorType.description: Filter by investor type: 'person' for individuals, 'organization' for firms, 'either' to include both types. Pass null/undefined to include both types (same as 'either')

types.description: Investor types filter with include/exclude pattern. Examples: include: ['venture_capital', 'angel']

numLeadInvestments.description: Filter by number of lead investments (range)

leadRate.description: Filter by lead investment rate (0.0 - 1.0). Values outside [0,1] will be clamped to this range

lastInvestmentDate.description: Filter by last investment date (date range)

foundedOn.description: Filter by founded date (date range). Note: many investors have null founded_on, so this filter may exclude many rows

field.description: Field to sort by. For 'importance', higher values indicate a more important investor (as determined by our AI). Use DESC direction to start with the most important investors.

direction.description: Sort order - asc or desc (defaults to desc)

sorting.description: Sorting configuration. If provided, field is required. Defaults to total_investment_count DESC if not provided.

isTopVc.description: Filter by whether investor is a top VC. Note: only a few dozen companies qualify as 'top'

domains.description: Array of investor domains to filter by

namePatterns.description: An array of name patterns used for partial, case-insensitive matching against investor names

crunchbaseSlugs.description: Array of Crunchbase slugs to filter by (exact match). Slugs are the last part of the Crunchbase URL (for example, 'sequoia-capital' from crunchbase.com/organization/sequoia-capital)

pageSize.description: Number of investors to return per page (max 1000)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor returned from a previous search response. Use this to fetch the next page of results. Null for the first page.

x-credit-cost.description: 3 credits per investor found

name.description: Investor name

type.description: Investor type: 'person' for individuals, 'organization' for firms

isTopVc.description: Whether investor is a top VC

domain.description: Investor domain

shareOfPortfolio.description: Share of total investments accounted for by this stage (0.0 - 1.0)

lastInvestmentDate.description: Date of most recent investment at this stage

orgLinkedinSlug.description: LinkedIn slug for the organization invested in (in long format, e.g., 'company/companyname')

orgName.description: Name of the organization invested in

roundType.description: Type of funding round (e.g., series_a, seed)

wasLeadInvestor.description: Whether the investor was lead investor

investors.description: Array of investors matching the search criteria

nextCursor.description: Cursor for fetching the next page of results. Null if no more results available

post.description: Search for investments with flexible filtering capabilities. Supports filtering by investor, company, round type, location, and financial metrics. ⚡ Rate limit: 30 requests per 1 minute 💰 Cost: 2 credits per investment found ⓘ

investorName.description: Filter by investor name (partial match, case-insensitive). Use ILIKE pattern matching.

investorType.description: Filter by investor type: 'person' for individuals, 'organization' for firms, 'either' to include both types. Pass null/undefined to include both types (same as 'either')

investorCountryCode.description: Investor country or region filter with include/exclude pattern. Region codes like X-ANGLOSPHERE expand to multiple countries (USA, GBR, AUS, etc.)

investorDomain.description: Filter by investor domain (exact match). For example, 'techstars.com' to find all investments made by Techstars

investorCrunchbaseSlug.description: Filter by investor Crunchbase slug (exact match). For example, 'sequoia-capital' to find all investments made by Sequoia Capital

companyName.description: Filter by company name (partial match, case-insensitive). Use ILIKE pattern matching.

companyLinkedinUrl.description: Filter by company LinkedIn URL. Accepts either full URL (e.g., 'https://www.linkedin.com/company/fiber-ai/') or slug (e.g., 'fiber-ai'). You can also use LinkedIn organization ID in the slug (e.g., 'https://www.linkedin.com/company/123456789/' or just '123456789')

roundType.description: Round type filter with include/exclude pattern. Examples: include: ['series_a', 'seed']

roundDate.description: Filter by funding round announcement date (date range)

raisedAmountUSD.description: Filter by amount raised in USD (range). For example, min: 5000000 to find rounds with at least $5M raised

postMoneyValuationUSD.description: Filter by post-money valuation in USD (range)

countryCode.description: Round location country or region filter with include/exclude pattern. Region codes like X-ANGLOSPHERE expand to multiple countries (USA, GBR, AUS, etc.)

stateName.description: Round location state name filter with include/exclude pattern. Use full state names (e.g., 'California', 'England', 'New York'). State codes like 'CA' or 'NY' are not accepted.

city.description: Filter by round location city (partial match, case-insensitive). Use ILIKE pattern matching.

wasLeadInvestor.description: Filter by whether the investor was a lead investor in the round

minInvestorCount.description: Filter by minimum number of investors in the funding round

direction.description: Sort order - asc or desc (defaults to desc)

sorting.description: Sorting configuration. If provided, field is required. Defaults to round_date DESC if not provided.

searchParams.description: Investment search filter parameters. If not provided, returns all investments (subject to pagination)

pageSize.description: Number of investments to return per page (max 100)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor returned from a previous search response. Use this to fetch the next page of results. Null for the first page.

x-credit-cost.description: 2 credits per investment found

roundType.description: Type of funding round (e.g., series_a, seed)

name.description: Company name

domain.description: Company domain

linkedinUrl.description: Full LinkedIn URL for the company. Examples: 'https://www.linkedin.com/company/fiber-ai/' or 'https://www.linkedin.com/company/123456789/' (using organization ID)

linkedinId.description: LinkedIn organization ID

name.description: Investor name

linkedinUrl.description: Full LinkedIn URL for the investor. Examples: 'https://www.linkedin.com/in/johndoe/' for person or 'https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-capital/' for organization. You can also use organization ID (e.g., 'https://www.linkedin.com/company/123456789/')

isLeadInvestor.description: Whether the investor was a lead investor

countryCode.description: Country code where the funding round occurred

state.description: State where the funding round occurred

city.description: City where the funding round occurred

roundLocation.description: Location where the funding round occurred

investorCount.description: Total number of investors in this funding round

nextCursor.description: Pagination cursor for the next page. Null if there are no more results.

Starts fetching a single person's contact details: personal email, work email, and/or phone number. This is an asynchronous task; use the polling endpoint afterward. ⚡ Rate limit: 120 requests per 1 minute 💰 Cost: Pay only for the data you request:
• 5 credits for all phone numbers AND all emails (12 credits if exhaustive)

post.description:
Streamlined synchronous contact reveal. Only requires a LinkedIn URL — profile details are resolved automatically. Uses a faster enrichment stack. For hyper-speed requirements, consider using the turbo endpoint instead. ⚡ Rate limit: 200 requests per 1 minute 💰 Cost: Pay only for the data you request:
• 5 credits for all phone numbers AND all emails
• 2 credits for work email only
• 2 credits for personal email only
• 3 credits for phone only ⓘ

post.description: Given an email address, find the person's LinkedIn profile and personal details. If you also have the person's name, company, or other identifiers, use the Kitchen Sink endpoint instead — it accepts all available signals and produces better matches. **Rate limit**: 50 requests per second. To avoid 429 errors, space requests evenly (~20ms apart) rather than bursting them all at once. Bursting can trigger the fixed-window rate limiter even below the stated limit. ⚡ Rate limit: 50 requests per 1 second 💰 Cost: 2 credits per email lookup ⓘ

email.description: The email address of a person whose LinkedIn profile you want to find.

x-credit-cost.description: 2 credits per email lookup

data.description: LinkedIn profiles matching this email, ordered by best match. Usually contains one result.

rejection.description: Present when the email is unresolvable (e.g. disposable, anonymous relay, or role-based address). The request succeeded but there is no person to find.

strategy.description: The strategy by which to search for places.

x-credit-cost.description: 3 credits per business found

searchID.description: The ID of the Google Maps search. Pass this to the check and poll endpoints to monitor progress and retrieve results.

validationScore.description: Validation score from 0-10 (10 = valid and reachable, 6 = valid but not reachable, 2 = unknown, 0 = invalid)

post.summary: Search companies and prospects from text

Takes free-form text (e.g., 'Senior Product Managers from Series A to C FinTech startups in New York') and produces standardized filters (industries, funding stages, headcount ranges, locations, titles, seniorities, etc.). When limits are provided, executes the search and returns matching companies and people in a single synchronous call. ⚡ Rate limit: 20 requests per 1 minute 💰 Cost: Fixed costs per request:

city.description: The city where the company is located.

address.description: The street address of the company.

findEmailDomains.description: Whether to find the email domains of the company.

otherContext.description: Any other disambiguating information or identifiers for the company, such as "YC startup" or "Italian airline"

description.description: A description of the company, such as what it does or its industry focus.

companyInfo.description: List of companies to look up. Max 400 companies can be provided at a time.

post.description: Convert GitHub usernames to LinkedIn profiles or get emailIds. Use outputType flag to specify what to retrieve. ⚡ Rate limit: 10 requests per 1 minute 💰 Cost: 1 credit per person for LinkedIn lookup OR email extraction. 2 credits per person if outputType='both'. ⓘ

overallContext.description: Descriptor of what your people have in common and who they are. For example: "Engineers at Fiber AI" or "YC founders 2021 batch." Helps our agent disambiguate people with similar names.

people.description: List of GitHub usernames to look up. Maximum 1000 people can be provided at a time.

outputType.description: What to extract from GitHub profile. 'linkedin' finds LinkedIn profile, 'email' extracts work emails from commits, 'both' returns both.

x-credit-cost.description: 1 credit per person for LinkedIn lookup OR email extraction. 2 credits per person if outputType='both'.

githubAgentRunId.description: The ID of the GitHub to LinkedIn lookup run. Provide this to the polling endpoint to get actual results back.
